svg.title: Email Warm-Up Ramp
svg.desc: Email warm-up ramp showing how to gradually increase sending volume as a percentage of target volume over six or more weeks. The process is stepped, not continuous — volume is held at each level while engagement is observed before increasing. Phase 1 (weeks 1–2) seeds initial trust at 2–5% of target volume, requiring open rates above 30%. Phase 2 (weeks 3–4) establishes reputation at 10–25% of target volume. Phase 3 (weeks 4–5) scales to 50–75% as reputation solidifies. Phase 4 (week 6+) reaches full target volume. At every step, volume should only increase when engagement metrics hold steady — if engagement drops, hold or reduce volume. Ramping too fast without engagement causes reputation collapse and spam folder placement that takes weeks to recover from. Seed engagement from tools like InboxAlly accelerates trust building during the critical early phases.

cc:Work.dc:title: Email Warm-Up Ramp Diagram
cc:Work.dc:creator: InboxAlly
cc:Work.dc:subject: email warm-up, domain warm-up, IP warm-up, sending volume ramp, engagement rate, open rate, sender reputation, inbox placement, spam folder, reputation recovery, seed engagement, warm-up schedule, email deliverability
cc:Work.dc:description: Stepped ramp diagram showing phased volume increases during email warm-up, with engagement thresholds, phase goals, and warnings about ramping too fast.

g.aria-label: Stepped volume ramp showing gradual increase from 2% to 100% of target volume over 6 weeks
Warning: ramping volume too fast without engagement causes reputation collapse
Phase 1: Seed — weeks 1 and 2, establish baseline trust with most engaged subscribers
g.data-description: Send to most engaged subscribers at 2-5% of target volume to establish baseline trust

g.aria-label: Phase 2: Establish — weeks 3 and 4, build consistent engagement history
g.data-description: Expand to broader engaged segments at 15-35% to build consistent history

g.aria-label: Phase 3: Scale — weeks 5 and 6+, reach full target volume
g.data-description: Scale to full list at 65-100% once reputation is proven

g.aria-label: Engagement rate thresholds: open rate must stay above these levels before increasing volume
Key rule: only increase volume when engagement metrics hold steady. If engagement drops, hold or reduce volume.
g.text: If open rate drops → hold volume or reduce until it recovers

g.aria-label: InboxAlly seed engagement accelerates trust building during early warm-up phases
g.text: Consistent positive signals help establish trust faster during warm-up

g.aria-label: Phase guidance: what to send to and what to watch for at each phase
